title: Grewia miqueliana
intro: Grewia miqueliana, also known as the Java hawthorn, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. Traditionally, Grewia miqueliana has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Grewia miqueliana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Grewia miqueliana is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat a range of health issues, including coughs, colds, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Grewia miqueliana is classified under the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. This family consists of over 200 genera and 2,300 species, and is widely distributed across the globe. As a member of the Grewia genus, Grewia miqueliana is closely related to other species within this group, which are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Grewia itself is composed of around 250 species, many of which are native to Africa and Asia. Grewia miqueliana is a species within this diverse and widespread genus.
